review_text: As a tour operator, I rely on fair and accurate reviews to build trust with customers. Recently, I’ve had two separate cases that raised serious concerns about how review moderation is handled.In one instance, a customer attempted to obtain a refund outside of policy and indicated they would leave a negative review and use social media to damage our business after it was declined. In another, a guest engaged in inappropriate behaviour during an experience, was removed to ensure the safety of others, and then left a negative review afterwards.Both cases were reported properly with supporting evidence. However, the reviews remain visible, and decisions appear to be based primarily on the content of the review itself, without considering the surrounding context such as customer conduct, prior communication, or enforcement of safety rules.From an operator’s perspective, this creates a gap in protection. Reviews influenced by disputes, pressure, or inappropriate behaviour can still impact businesses, even when policies have been followed correctly.I fully support honest feedback, but there should also be clearer mechanisms to ensure that context is taken into account in cases like these.

My business of 11 years moved. It is a by appointment only. I changed the address in Trip Advisor. Same business, same website just new location. The address did not fully change. I called for support.Tech said he had to close the business and restart a new one. Unbelievable! In this day and age, they can't make a change in location to a business. I am sure businesses move all the time...He said how would you feel if you went to a business that was no longer there!?  I told him that we are by appointment only so no one would be coming without an appointment....goodbye Trip Advisor! Not worth my troubles. I would give you a zero star review as a small business owner.

I left a review about a really dangerous hotel "casa santo domingo" They opened fire on students and engaged in political acts that endangered both guests and neighbors.I left potos and the event was on the news, easy too google itAnyways wasnt approved.
Horrible! They messed up my tickets to the colosseum so I could not go. Once in a lifetime trip. Do not trust them. I will NEVER use again for anything.
I had 2 recent bad experiences with Trip Advisors. I will look for other platforms in the future.

ATTN:  USAA Members:  We had travel insurance with TRAVEL INSURED INTERNATIONAL (TII) but had to cancel the trip due to my urgent hospitalization.  Although we submitted the claim paperwork with all required documentation to TII on 21 March 2026, and received by "Leo Glynn" at TII's claim department (no date indicated), they have delayed refunding our payments for the trip as of today's date (17 April 2026) stating they needed additional documentation from USAA Member Travel Privileges.  None of these additional documents were required by the original claim form documents.  I spoke with TII representative Kris this morning.  She stated they received the required documentation on 2 April but the claim has still not been processed due to a large backlog.  She was unable to suggest a date when the claim might might be processed and a refund submitted,

Useful platform for checking traveler photos and recent reviews. The filters make it easy to narrow down options for hotels and restaurants. A bit busy with ads, but the information is generally reliable for planning trips.

Purchased Vatican Museum and Sistine Chapel Tickets from Tripadvisor. Paid 4 times the face value but this was a must see for our family. Went to get tickets in Rome and was told that they were cancelled. VERY disappointed.Don't book through Tripadvisor.

I am unable to look at resturants, locations, hotels without the page jumping to the search screen. Every time I click an item it just jumps to the search screen and so it is totaally unusable.There is no contact for tech issues and no help on the help pages at all.

Tripadvisor took booking for a tour of Auschwitz, charged my card and sent me a confirmation.  On the day of the tour when I messaged operator to find out where the van was that was supposed to pick us up the operator calls me and said Tripadvisor made a mistake and never should have taken the booking.  This was important for family reasons.  I took my daughter with for this tour.  When will we make it back, if ever?

We are a tourism operator based in Malta and have recently encountered a serious issue with Tripadvisor that remains unresolved. We relocated from one area of Malta to another (7km) and instead of marking our listing "moved to another location", they have marked our listing as “Permanently Closed,” which is factually incorrect, harmful to our business and highly misleading to potential customers.Tripadvisor previously approved similar address updates for us (greater distance), but now applies its rules inconsistently, leaving false information online.We have raised this as a formal issue under the EU Digital Services Act and are escalating this through formal regulatory channels.

Trip Advisor is an excellent place to read reviews about food establishments etc, but it would help if Trip Advisor displayed food establishments hygiene ratings on their website.So many businesses on the Isle of Wight that score poor scores do not display their hygiene ratings, so how do customers visiting know they are not the cleanest food establishments, otherwise Trip advisor is a great idea.

Trip Advisor asked me to finish my review and then did not offer a submit button. And there is no way to contact Trip Advisor directly to make a specific inquiry. The FAQ and other sections are no help at all.

I spent two nights over the werkend at the Gillen HouseB & B in Maxey’s, Ga near Athens. The 1920’s home has been beautifully restored with delightful furniture and fun side pieces and beautiful artwork. I loved my bedroom!!The Hostess is a wonderful artist and interior designer.  I loved sitting on the upstairs screened porch listening in the evenings to the fountain and watching the goldfish in the pond.  Both the breakfasts and dinners were delicious and very creative. Both the host and the hostess were very helpful with me as I had a wound problem on my leg.  They were also very fun people with whom to spend time with. Many fun stories to tell. It was just the retreat that I needed. Thank you.
Tripadvisor is a horrible company. They keep bad reviews for years and won't remove them, after issues have been addresses.

We had a tour of the Danube booked through Trip Advisor. Mandatory prepay with no refunds. Drove from Bucharest to Tulcea, stayed overnight in a hotel to insure we were at the dock prior to our 0 AM departure. Arrived at the dock and “no show” tour boat. We went to the office at the dock and were told no Danube boat tours were scheduled on that day. So, we wasted gas and hotel costs for nothing. Trip Advisor did refund our tour fees, but hardly a compensation for the frustration. Poor job of vetting operators by Trip Advisor.

I have always looked at the reviews on Tripadvisor and, before booking a holiday at the  5* Baron Palms Resort in Sharm el Sheikh, we looked at the reviews The reviews were mainly excellent so we booked this hotel. The reality was very different - at best a 4* hotel. We spoke with many people during our stay and everyone agreed it was a 4* hotel at best. When I returned home I looked at the reviews given at the time we stayed there. A large number of very short ‘excellent’ reviews were posted by non English guests, the wording of some were very similar. This was very surprising because almost all the guests were English. Since returning the hotel has now gone up to 8th best hotel in SharmI contacted the CEO of Tripadvisor and no one from his company acknowledged either of my 2 emails. Given the premise of their business is based on honesty and truth - this does not bode well

I stayed at Eden Jasmine Resort & Spa Hotel in Hammamet Tunisia, and had a fabulous experience. All the staff were so friendly and helpful. The hotel is safe; I have never stayed at a hotel where I was familiar with the majority of the staff. Amiri (excursions) will help you and give full details of the excursions he always prioritises your safety. If I ever go back to Tunisia I would definitely go back to Eden Jasmine Resort &Spa Hotel. 10/10 chef's kiss.

I never felt ripped off before by Trip Advisor. I should have known better. 100% mark up on tour tickets to Alcatraz in SF. I was in a hurry to book and assumed they’d be fair. Wrong. Deleted the app. Arrivederci!

DO NOT BOOK ANY TOUR WITH TRIPADVISOR VIATOR.NO WAY TO CONTACT EITHER COMPANY WHEN THINGS GO WRONG.Booked a December 07 2025 trip via Tripadvior Viator to Blenden Castle in England. We received confirmation and got to Victoria Station London for the Tour. The operator Grayline had representatives there assisting on a number of tours. When we got to the assigned meeting point the bus was for a Harry Potter tour. We asked the representative from Grayline and they said that particular tour had been cancelled week before.  We showed them our VIATOR confirmation and they tried to look us up. They had no record of this trip having ever to be booked. They did have a record of a previous trip the year before to Stonehenge…..As I wrote this trip was for December 7 and the ccd charge for this trip was processed by Viator on December 5. Even with evidence the trip never took place nor that we were ever booked by Viator on any Grayline tour, Viator still processed the charge and refuses a refund. Tripadvior is also useless in resolution.Viator politely send a reply stating they wanted to make it right and gave us options to contact them but it is impossible to use phone numbers provided, advice for a live chat or response to emails to customer service at Viator….there has been no reply. IN FACT, when we disputed the charge with our CCD company Viator still stated the tour was booked.

i left a negative review for a city my wife and I visited.  TripAdvisor sent me an email with a link to their new guidelines that, for all intents and purposes, disallows negative reviews. The incredibly long, lawyer-created, cleverly-worded policy even prohibits any review that is 'biased'.  LOL!!!!  ALL REVIEWS ARE BIASED!!! THAT'S WHAT A REVIEW IS!!! A review is YOUR relating of YOUR opinion biased to YOUR experience of a place or thing. Different people have different experiences and their opinions are biased to their respective experiences.  that one person's experience was negative does not make it less valuable or less authentic than the person who had a good experience. TripAdvisor does not allow the opinions of those who had objectively bad experiences with a place or a service.  The irony is thick enough to cut with a chainsaw - their ENTIRE ONLINE SERVICE IS NOW BIASED TOWARDS THE ENTITIES BEING REVIEWED! LOL!  you will not be able to get a well-rounded, whole-truth review of any entity on TripAdvisor.
I wrote a review and it wasn’t admitted. It’s clear that Tripadvisor is not as transparent as it wish to be. Another empty box.

Awful website which i will not use again to leave reviews. Apparently my photos were taken from elsewhere which is hilarious. Im probably the only adult in the world that doesnt use social media and as for ai I have no idea why anyone would use this. Ridiculous and will be deleting account. As clearly pointless.

Ocean key resort by visaRooms are small but clean & very pleasant employees.  The activities director, Joanna Michele was fantastic and very knowledgeable . Very fun crafts at no cost that are worth taking home!!

I wrote a detailed review about a trip I was on. First, they removed all my photos because they decided it wasn't relevant for the trip - not sure how communication showing how what was promised was not delivered is not relevant. Also, invoices showing that we were overcharged were deemed not to be relevant. Then eventually they removed my review altogether saying it went against some of their terms. I emailed them a few times asking what terms I actually violated since I couldn't find anything in their policy against what I wrote and I wanted to give the full story - not just leave out random parts. They simply replied with their decision is final. Now after providing proof that someone attempted to scam me - especially since that person used the positive reviews on TripAdvisor to get me in - they decided that the scammer should rather get off scot-free.

If I can give them a NEGATIVE STAR, I would.  Prior to engaging online with Tripadvisor, I did my research to check the price of the specific hotel that I'd like to stay.  It was my very first time to use online for booking a hotel reservaton and it happened that I used them.  The online reservation I made was almost 4 weeks ahead of time of the intended check-in date.  I received just a few minutes after from an associate for me to make the payment.  The total amount I need to pay was verified over the phone and I proceed making the payment.  Few minutes after, I received an email confirmation with the total amount BUT it was MORE THAN what I was told over the phone.  My credit card was charged for more than the amount I was informed over the phone.  Within about 10 minutes from the time we were on the phone, the time I received the email and the time I called back, I tried asking for a cancellation and full refund.  The reason behind this is that my credit card reflect additional charges, and these are the TAX and RESORT FEES.  The hotel I was trying to book through them is in asia. I stayed in this hotel several time where I used to call them directly to make reservation.  Anyhow, long story short, I asked to cancel and a full refund but they only refunded me partial of the amount they charged my card.  I was charged with A HEFTLY AMOUND OF CANCELLATION FEE, I only saw this fee after they sent me the invoice via email. It was not disclosed over the phone.  HORRIBLE ONLINE SERVICE....AVOID THIS SITE!

The villa was clean and well kept. The pool was a little too cold to be in my comfort range! The employees were friendly and did a good job,  I was especially impressed with Rosa at the front desk!! She was a gem, friendly, helpful and so willing to take the time to deal with any problems a guest might have!
A site that takes money from businesses for taking bad reviews away is 100% not to be trusted.Only popular because it got in first.As bigger scam as some of the businesses that pay it.

I booked a horseback riding trip in Argentina that was canceled by the operator. I then called TripAdvisor by phone to schedule a different horseback riding trip. The representative scheduled a new trip and unbeknownst to me was in a different city 8 hrs away! I don't know the terrain and was not aware of how far it was away. They finally gave me a credit for the trip after calling multiple times. This was their fault. Then they kicked me off their site. I can no longer use the platform and they were the ones that made the mistake.  I now use Get Your Guide. Much better company
Woeful. Accept reviews from fraudulent accounts. Should be shut down.

Trip advisor refuses to publish my GENUINE review about Youth Station Hostel - Rome and as it seems, they refuse to publish every negative review about this hostel acting in their favor without any excuse!! STAY AWAY FROM THEM!!

I submitted a review on their website for a company I booked a tour with in Nov 2025. My review was instantly flagged and not published because it was "not current". After raising a ticket, I received an email confirmation letting me know the review was now published. Clicked on the link, and no review, of course. Raised another ticket, and this time, was told  that the very same review they claimed they published, was now unpublished because of profanity. After more than ten email exchanges with this so called "user-generated content" operator, its sure to say their support is just bonkers, flavored with incompetence and pure stupidity.

They deserve a minus 10 stars. Their app is broken and they don’t care. It booked me in a room 10 days in advance instead of that night. I wouldn’t even be in the state then. I called to rebook for that night and they had a non-English speaking customer service rep deny my repeated attempts to rebook or refund my money. Trip Advsor tricked me  and cost me money

Corrupted company. They didnt post my review for. Ery dangerous property on bookings.comMany clients complained and think that this property is dangerous and dirty. Tripadvisor didnt update my review. Thye ve been paid.

DO NOT DO NOT DO NOT USE THEM FOR ANY BOOKINGS! I have been trying for weeks to get a refund on an excursion that was canceled due to the weather. They issued me a $66 refund out of $318. I have multiple documents saying I was due for a full refund and also many emails from them saying they would issue it and did not! They are a fraudulent company and at this point I am going to escalate this to small claims court. I highly suggest you document everything if something goes wrong on your trip. This is unacceptable and also unfair. I have used them for years and it seems like they take advantage of the people who make their business run.

Miramare Restaurant – Naples, FloridaValentine’s Day 2026Other than the beautiful waterfront view, there’s nothing to recommend about Miramare.My friend and I booked it for a special Valentine’s evening with our wives. Instead, it was a disappointment from start to finish.My wife ordered salmon — it was fine. I ordered the $56 Short Rib Special with risotto. The risotto was overcooked and pasty, completely flavorless. The “short ribs” were shredded pot roast — neither short nor ribs, and visually unappealing. My friend’s chicken parm came with overcooked spaghetti. His wife ordered the same short rib special and had the same bland result.We sent both short rib plates back and reordered. Our replacements arrived 45 minutes later — after our spouses had already finished their meals. So much for a romantic dinner.The manager apologized and promised complimentary cocktails that never appeared.Slow service. Poor execution. Overcooked pasta in an Italian restaurant — in Naples.A pretty view isn’t enough.Never again.

This is a beautiful wine farm and we enjoyed the meal we had in the garden. But I found using the toilets quite offputting. All the toilets are for both male and female and each time I went, I came out with the bottom of my shoes covered in urine. An experience preferably not to be repeated!

We were loyal customers for 8 years. We planned and booked many trips through them. One time, we made a simple mistake and booked a Spanish-language tour instead of English. The cost was about $50. Not a big amount, but we had to take a 1-hour train each way to attend the tour.We informed the guide that we do not speak Spanish. He made zero effort to help and later promised a refund. That refund never happened.What followed was even worse. Customer service showed no interest in solving the issue. No responsibility. No respect for loyal customers. One small mistake was handled horribly from start to finish.If this company wants to grow, they should seriously learn from Amazon’s customer service approach.We are closing our old profile and moving to other booking platforms. I do not recommend this one.

Avoid at all costs. They only publish good reviews. We recently took a three night trip to San Blas that was highly rated. In reality there was no communication between travelers, drivers, accommodations, etc. Nothing was as described. Only through the kindness of one captain and a transport driver were we able to get back to Panama City. Sadly San Blas islands have become a tourist dumping ground. Look for reviews outside of trip advisor

Today I received for me to check in my reservation at the American Hotel, and I was not going to be able to make it. I called and was assisted by Leeha. She was able to cancel my reservations with no penalty. She was no nice and i want to say a big thank you from fellow team to another!

Welcoming atmosphere. Arriving on the drive .valet greeted me with a warm smile ready to serve. Check in was fast . Unsure how to work the lights in the bathroom. I called the front desk . A very bubbly n professional but friendly lady answered her name was Kia she helped my work the hotel fancy light system. Kia also took my breakfast order the next morning. I will for sure tell friends and family where to stay when they visit

DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY. They canceled our reservation last minute so we showed up with no idea we wouldn't have a ride. Then they did not refund the entire amount, so are currently waiting and trying to contact someone for our money back with currently no luck.

As a business owner, I find TripAdvisor to be one of the least reliable platforms when it comes to unbiased reviews. Their review policies appear inconsistently applied, if at all. We have received clearly fake reviews from competitors, and despite providing documented proof, TripAdvisor has refused to remove them.Even more concerning, we have seen reviews posted by individuals who have never dined at our restaurant, which completely undermines the platform’s credibility. There seems to be no effective verification process, no real moderation, and no protection for businesses against false or malicious content.Because of these repeated issues, I no longer consider TripAdvisor a trustworthy source for reviews. In contrast, platforms like OpenTable, which verify reviews through actual reservations, are among the few reliable review sources left.

BEWARE!!!! SCAMMM! We booked via TripAdvisor transportation from Nassau AirPort to Atlantis for 1/30/26 @ 5pm. When booking received it had been changed to 2/2/26 @ 5pm. Contacted TripAdvisor customer service and they advised there was no availability on 1/30/26 so they AUTOMATICALLY adjusted the reservation to 2/2/26. I advised that was unacceptable and not what we originally requested. Agent just kept parroting that there are no changes or refunds and refused to get a supervisor.  BEWARE!!!

I’m a big fan of TripAdvisor hotel reviews.  Unfortunately, I made a hotel reservation on their app.  No confirmation e-mail, nothing on my account page, no nothing.  Credit card was charged.  AI search produced a help telephone number ( nowhere on the app.). 20 minutes, 2 agents ( both offshore), promised to email confirmation.  Nada.  Finales called the hotel itself and they sent confirmation email.  Looks like TripAdvisor just fobs off hotel reservations to other travel apps.  Don’t waste your time actually trying to book something on their site.
!!!! so many fake reviews never delete

The appliances are not serviced as the don’t work ie: fridge/ tv cupboards doors flimsy. Rooms not cleaned properly.Staff very rude and aggressive. With threatening behaviour Over charging on services or exchange rates. Not honest.It’s in the middle of nowhere and will cost you an arm or a leg each time you go down the road to do shopping or night out. Absolutely mind blowing experience or purchasing a nightmare.
I do not want my business on TripAdvisor. A customer reviewed us,and whilst i haven't  claimed the site. It is virtually impossible to de activate.Fascist doesn't quite cover this set up.

Crap company to book any experience through - booking system that doesn't retain data, nobody who can help you - and no possible human contact to fix issues❌ No reliable case submission❌ Circular help pages that don't help❌ App out of sync with email and loses data

Terrible. So uninterested in listening to our issues and problems with some reviews that are dating 10 years back. So completely closing the doors infront of our eyes. First they gave us tons of work to get a solution on our problem and when I gave them what they wanted they still didn’t even wanna read. So said that this multimillion billion company have stolen the market so completely. Makes me so said that they want to hurt and destroy small businesses.

Trip advisor are not a fair mediator of reviews. A vendor is in an impossible situation where a review can be completely fake with a completely fake name but they are under no obligation to prove their claims so the vendor has to put up and shut up. Where is the fairness in that?

Once upon a time, long ago, TripAdvisor was actually useful—a place you could trust to choose a restaurant or a business.Today? It’s more like a creative writing platform.Anyone with five minutes, a fake email, and a bad mood can post a “review” about a place they’ve never stepped into. No reservation, no bill, no proof—just imagination and resentment. And voilà: instant critic.What’s truly impressive is not how unreliable it has become, but that it still exists… and that people still take it seriously.

I booked JW Marriotte Hotel Marina through tripadvisor. Fully paid a week ago and have confirmation number. I fly to Dubai with my wife and I was told, my name can not be found. The Hotel is sold out. Tripadvisor told me to wait for an hour to work with Hotel partner, and came back appologized and ask another hour. What is absurd is they offer me a hotel 30km away. I used to like Tripadvisor, but never again after my horrible experience. Finally canceled as I know they will keep me whole day. Shame!

Booked a holiday based on TripAdvisor rating and realised 90% of the reviews were fake when we got to the hotel. Disappointed in TripAdvisor lack of ability or desire to ensure reviews are legitimate or use a simple weighting system to put more weight on people who have made multiple reviews.
Trip advisor let's people that haven't stayed at a property to write reviews. They also let properties who pay them for a service to remove bad reviews. Read the reviews with a grain of salt.

I wrote a very negative review of a tour company that TripAdvisor had recommended.  Their bot thanked me for the review but it never appeared.  They "whitewash" their reviews! Don't trust a word they say!

Months in advance, I booked a tour from Buenos Aires to Colonia del Sacramento through TripAdvisor, operated by Colonia Express.We only received a voucher, which clearly stated that it was not the ticket and that the ticket should be collected at the time of boarding. The problem is that, upon arriving at the indicated location and presenting all the information provided by TripAdvisor, there was simply no ticket or reservation linked to our name.Because of this, we were not allowed to board, completely losing the tour. Our entire travel itinerary was organized around this tour, which ended up being wasted.It is unclear whether:• TripAdvisor only sold a "reservation" without generating the tickets, or• there was a communication failure with Colonia Express.The fact is that we received absolutely no guidance on how to collect the tickets, where to do so, or who to contact. I have used TripAdvisor before, and normally the guides contact you the day before explaining the entire procedure—which did not happen this time. The voucher we received was useless, and the reservation, in practice, didn't exist.Furthermore, we were in another country, with communication difficulties, and we were treated hostilely on site. Even in this situation, the refund was denied.Another serious issue was the time provided:The voucher stated that the boarding deadline was 8:30 am, with a recommendation to arrive 1 hour earlier. We arrived at 7:40 am, within the indicated time, and were informed that boarding and the boat had already departed. In other words, the information provided was completely inconsistent.The tour is poorly explained, not intuitive at all, there is no adequate support on site, and in the end, the consumer is left completely helpless. We lost an entire day of travel, in addition to the money paid, for a reservation made months ago that was not honored.Given all these shortcomings, I request:• A full refund of the amount paid,• and a formal explanation of what happened with our reservation.The minimum expected is respect for the consumer and responsibility in the sale of tourism services.

They are removing negative reviews of a resort I was recently at. When I ask why, they say it violated guidelines, but cannot say which one. Funny enough all the positive ones if the resort stay, but magically the negative ones disappear. TripAdvisor is a scam.

I had a very disappointing experience with Tripadvisor during a trip to Egypt, a destination where tourists unfortunately need to be extra cautious due to the high number of scams.An operator on the platform falsely claimed that I participated in a tour, which I did not. In reality, the operator asked me to cancel the booking — I have WhatsApp conversations clearly proving this. Despite providing this evidence, the claim that the tour took place was still used against me.I requested concrete proof that I actually joined the tour, but instead of proper customer protection, the responsibility was shifted back to me as the customer. This kind of handling creates an environment where dishonest operators can take advantage of travelers.To make this absolutely clear, I completed a tour the following day with a different operator, and I have proof of that experience as well.Given the known risks in Egypt, I expected Tripadvisor to be more proactive in protecting customers. Unfortunately, that was not my experience. I strongly advise travelers to document everything and proceed with caution when using this platform.

I closed a cruise tour for tomorrow, December 31. I had already paid for everything, me, my wife and my children. The company contacted me today, 1 day before the tour, to charge me extra - 750 dollars per person. An absurd and total lack of respect for the tourist. TripAdvisor should honor the tour with the tour operator.

Reviews are well out of date and do not reflect if the business is still operating. Currently in Hua Hin Thailand and reviews for most places are over ten years old. Anything over two years need removing as it's not showing current activity and status of the plac

My poor review is based entirely on having to watch three men being intimate at their table. Their disgraceful behaviour spoiled my enjoyment of the experience. When I pointed out my dissatisfaction and disgust to the manageress, she apologised but did nothing. They claim to uphold high standards. I’ve never seen anyone behave so badly in a public restaurant. So, expect quality food, but also any kind of anti social behaviour.

⭐⭐ Radisson Barbados – Disappointing 10-Night StayWe stayed at the Radisson Barbados for 10 nights and unfortunately the experience was very disappointing.The breakfast buffet was extremely basic and felt more like a budget hotel than a Radisson. Every day it was largely the same: deep-fried bacon, scrambled eggs, sausages, beans, fish cakes, plantain, a few tiny pastries, melon, grapes, kiwi, and a couple of other fruits. There was a basic omelette station. After a long stay, the lack of variety became very frustrating. One member of staff in the breakfast area, Jeanette, really stood out as friendly and helpful — sadly she was the exception.Housekeeping was very poor. My sons were actually told off by cleaners for their room not being tidy — they are on holiday. Towels were not replaced, water was not restocked, and we were left without toilet roll on more than one occasion.The pool is extremely small, with no towels provided and not enough loungers. The beach area also lacked sufficient loungers. Most of the beach staff were rude and argumentative. One staff member named Shane stood out positively; the rest made interactions uncomfortable rather than welcoming.We also had a frustrating experience with food service at the beach. We ordered three pizzas to our sun loungers. There was confusion over the order, and we were told that “in their culture” a margherita is considered a vegetable pizza, despite our friends clearly ordering margherita, pepperoni, and vegetable. The staff member made a mistake but refused to acknowledge it or resolve the issue, which again came across as argumentative rather than customer-focused.The rooms are very basic, the beds are very small, and overall the rooms feel tired and outdated. To make matters worse, security accused my husband of lying about which room we were staying in, which was completely unacceptable and embarrassing.This hotel could be amazing, as the location is good and we hired cars to get around easily. However, the hotel needs a full refresh, and the staff urgently need proper training in all areas of hospitality.Overall, this was not the standard expected from the Radisson brand, and we would not return unless significant improvements are made

Been to Cappadocia Turkish restaurant in Warrington , I have to admit it was the best meal we ever had the service is great friendly staff meet the owner too and very passionate about his business I will highly recommend this place. Definitely will go back again .
Trip advisor absolute gutter of reviews No control people can make fake reviews with no checks

I have booked on this trip and i paid for it. somehow the owner of this trip has told us that their equipment could not support the trip and asked us to cancel the booking from our side. but until now the refund has not yet reflected. beware of this site. i really don't want to write a review. but there is not proper support where we can contact about the refund status and any update of it will refunded or not. basically this website support is not there. please don't use this anymore and avoid yourself future hassle on being worried about your money in the future. thank you.
Prices not as advertised on menu  told this was printing error  all menus need to be changed this is not a good look for any restaurant menu price 5€ more than on menu

TripAdvisor refused to post the following review on their website. Eurostar advertised on TripAdvisor that it was pet-friendly, but when I arrived with my poodle, I was told it wasn't. Besides the inconvenience of having to find a hotel at the last minute, they refused to refund my booking of over 500€. TripAdvisor submitted a refund request to Eurostar, including documentation showing their false advertising. I received a reply from TripAdvisor that “even with the documentation,” Eurostar would not issue a refund. Eurostar has since corrected its advertising on TripAdvisor, but I’m still out more than 500€ because of their mistake.TripAdvisor should refund me and take responsibility for what it advertises.

We stayed at the Sofitel Al Hamra Beach Resort in mid-November and would definitely stay there again if we come back to that area again.  My husband and I are senior citizens, and he uses a cane when walking.  The staff went overboard on trying to make everything easier for him to get around, the villa was beautiful and spacious, and everyone very friendly and accomodating.  We ate an absolutely delicious meal at the onsite restaurant and the breakfast the next morning was likewise delicious.  I needed to exchange some American dollars for dirhams before checkout and was told the hotel could do that and request at checkout.  I did so and the man at the desk quickly completed the exchange.  I would highly recommend this hotel for anyone visiting the area.   The entire staff is friendly, knowledgeable, and eager to help and the views from the restaurant and pool are beautiful.
Rubbish!!! 👎👎👎👎👎

Avoid this company — unauthorised subscription chargesI unexpectedly discovered recurring subscription charges from My Trip Online / Tripsorted that I did not knowingly sign up for, despite being an experienced traveller and ex-cabin crew.I only purchased airport fast-track once (£14), but was later billed £79 twice for a “membership” I never consented to, created no account for, and received no confirmation emails about.When challenged, the company initially refused a full refund and attempted to frame partial refunds as “full and final settlement” even though I did NOT accept their offer.It took bank intervention, a chargeback, and multiple escalations before the company finally agreed to cancel the subscription and refund the full amount.If you see unexpected charges or subscriptions from this company, contact your bank immediately.My bank (Starling) handled the dispute professionally and advised me throughout.Sharing this to help others avoid the same experience.

I had my Viator/Tripadvisor Experiences account suddenly terminated in August 2025 with no clear explanation, only a vague mention of “inconsistencies.” Despite being a fully licensed operator in Egypt with all required permits, insurance, and positive guest feedback, my account was shut down without warning.The most damaging part is that Viator withheld payments for tours I had already delivered. TripAdvisor’s review process is a sham, and my experience proves they’re more interested in protecting businesses than amplifying honest user feedback. I submitted a truthful review about a business, but TripAdvisor forced me to rewrite it four times, each time demanding changes that stripped away the facts until it was a watered-down lie. This isn’t moderation—it’s censorship, plain and simple.When I appealed their decision (Content ID: 1024225908), I got a canned email on August 21, 2025, from their so-called Support Team) claiming they’d “thoroughly reviewed” my case but were upholding their decision. No explanation, no transparency—just a brush-off. They even had the nerve to say I can’t appeal again, which screams of a system rigged to silence critics. It’s obvious TripAdvisor is cozying up to the businesses they claim to rate, likely for financial gain.This isn’t a platform for travelers; it’s a pay-to-play scheme that buries honest feedback. If you’re looking for real reviews, skip TripAdvisor and find platforms that don’t manipulate user voices to please their partners. Save yourself the frustration and don’t trust their rigged system.

I showed up to meet my obligation to participate in the presentation and was confused as to why my flight time was impacting my attendance. I was referred to a corporate supervisor and he added that we are not allowed to schedule the presentation on our checkout day. I expressed that none of this was shared with me during payment or booking and not included in the terms and conditions within my email confirmation. He said it would take 1-2 days to review the call recordings and that I should leave the presentation and go on about my day. I asked what the options would be if I didn't attend the presentation - he shared that I could be charged for the stay upon review of the recordings. Pay for the stay??? I showed up to the presentation and my flight time did not hinder my participation in the 90-minute presentation. This is ridiculous and deceptive!

I am shocked to see 1.9 stars.   I've used this company a few times when going on holiday, they have been brilliant.  When I went to Ansterdam I used Tripadvisor.  However, going to Egypt proved different.   My friend and I booked to go to Cairo on 23.07.25 and Luxor on 25.07.25.  The operator rang and cancelled it and booked it on 22.07.25.   However, my friend and I developed diarrhoea and vomiting and could not go 10 hr (after the 24 hour cancellation period).   I tried to contact the tour operator, but they were not interested and came to our hotel shouting down the phone asking us to come.   We couldn't leave the room for fear that we would have an accident or vomit on the way down to talk to him.He was still prepared to take us on the plane with vomiting and diarrhoea, at the risk of making all the other passengers on the plane sick.   He shouted "why you not coming?"  I told him why and he kept repeating 'why?'  This went on for about 5 minutes then I put the phone down as he became irate and started shouting in Arabic. About 5 minute later I got a call from someone I can only assume was his boss.   He said "You not coming yo Cairo, "I tell driver go?" Or words to that effect.  I said "We can't come  we have vomiting and diarrhoea."  He just repeated the words "I tell driver go."  I just said yes. He went.So we had lost our money.    We were still happy as we had a second trip to Luxor on Friday 25th July.  I rang and asked the operator about 10 hrs before we were due to go if he could send the itinerary.  He said we are trying to book the plane tickets.   We did not receive anything.   We received an email asking us if we would like to go to Cairo instead, having missed the trip on 23rd July  we jumped at the chance.  We were elated as thus was one of the main reasons for going to Egypt - to see the pyramids!  So we were now going to Cairo - yay!  However, all the excitement turned to another nightmare.   The operator Etro Tours rang about 10.30 pm while we were fast asleep and said tried to change the trip back to Luxor, to which we said "No."  He was very angry  shouting and screaming on the phone,  we were TERRIFIED.   I had to put the phone down.  We were both crying at that point.  We didn't expect them to pick us up, but went down at 4 am in the lobby of the hotel to wait.   Surprisingly, the operator came to pick us up.   He took us to the airport, with no tickets,  no itinerary,  a man came up to us and told us it would be £25 each for visas which we paid.   No receipt no nothing.   He assured us it was all on our passports.   We finally got to Cairo and were separated from the rest of the group (we travelled on the plane with).  A man called Gamal said "You are the VIPs!"  This was just another rouse to extract as much money from us as possible.  We told him what we wanted.  To see the pyramids etc.  Which he did.  We then asked for an itinerary.  We did get one, with 2 other passengers names and passport numbers on it.     It also stated on Tripadvisor the Nile cruise was 10 euros, but Gamal said it was 25 euros, but he was giving it to us for 10!We had a great time seeing the pyramids, riding a camel.  The lunch was great.  We were dropped off at the airport at 3.00 pm which the flight was 9.00 pm.  We rang and asked whether this was why the itinerary was not given to us.   The other passengers we travelled with came back around 6.00 pm - reasonable time.  The operator shouted and screamed at us saying why didn't we go to Luxor.  We reminded him that they were the ones who changed it.  Even when we got back to Sharm el Sheikh, it was not plain sailing.   The bus driver started shouting saying he didn't have ys on the trip book.  He did that in full view of the other passengers.  Very embarrassing.  I just said, "please can you just take us back to our hotel"  He looked at us menacingly and said "You need to pay more money."  My friend said, "He going to throw us off the bus in the middleof nowhere. "  Again  we were TERRIFIED and so frightened (we're in our 50s and 60s).  Finally he dropped us off at our hotel.We both burst into tears when we got to our room and hugged each other.   To say this was 'an adventure' is an understatement.  It was a terrifying ordeal.   I do not ever want to visit Egypt again.

We booked through Tripadvisor two tickets for 'Mount Gornergrat: Zermatt's Spectacular Summit Ticket' for June 23 2025. The price was $265.47 per person. The only thing the Tripadvisor booking company provided to us was the train ticket for the Gornergrat Railway. There was no other value add, meaning there was no guide, no audio guide, nothing. After we returned that evening from Mt Gornergrat, we discovered that we could have booked the EXACT same ticket and experience directly with the Gornergrat Railway for CHF 132 per person, or $165 at the exchange rate on June 23. We contacted Tripadvisor and asked them to refund the difference, per their low price guarantee: "We guarantee the lowest price on all 400,000+ experiences available to book on our site. If you find the same product cheaper online up until 48 hours after your trip starts we'll refund the difference.It's easy to do:- Gather your information- Provide your Tripadvisor Experiences Booking Number or Itinerary Reference Number and the proof of the lower price you found (compared with the price you paid through Tripadvisor).- Send it to us via email (the website has the eamil to use that we cannot post in this review with the documents, your name, and your home address.- We'll review. We'll examine your materials and get in touch as soon as we can.-  Receive your refund:  If everything checks out, you'll get a refund of the difference between the two prices.We completed everything listed above. In 5  follow up emails their customer support team insisted they could not access the Gornergrat Railway website and thus could not confirm the lower price. After I escalated to their Head of Customer Experience, they eventually told us they will not honor their low price guarantee on a fine print technicality buried a couple of layers down on the website that the price of the other website has to be in the same currency as the the currency you used to pay for the booking. After another email, they agreed to refund the difference for one of two tickets.Bottom line is to find the actual company that conducts the tour and buy direct.

I run a cooking school listed on Tripadvisor as an experience. Somehow the experience needs to be managed on a platform run by a sister company Viator. Guests book on the Viator or other connected platforms snd receive a confirmation from Viator by email. But their confirmation system is not linked to their product descriptions and if I change the details of my product the changes I make are not automatically reflected in the confirmations they send to guests. Examples - continuing to send guests to the wrong location after we moved and updated all details to the new location - offering guests a free pickup service when we had withdrawn the offer more than a year previously. We found out when guests accused us of cheating. In both cases I I have requested copies of the confirmations that that they send to guests so I can validate the information. The answer comes back that the confirmations contain confidential guest info (Clearly it’s to hard to delete or mask that)It takes forever to get customer service (supplier support) with the annoying recorded nasal voice telling you that by continuing on the phonecall you agree to their terms and conditions, as if you had a choice. When you get to the Manila call centre, their staff are trained in paraphrasing your question and not letting you correct their when they are wrong in their statements. And they don’t have authority to do anything and must pass on the details of your question or complaint. But the worst bit is the convoluted corporate structure they have which means it’s never clear if TripAdvisor or Viator are responsible. One point last year they did not pay me for 6 consecutive months for my bookings because they could not correctly route a payment to a bank in Hong Kong.
